name: School Mental Health Funding — State vs Federal Reliability
slug: school-mental-health-funding
status: active

related_entities: [samhsa, hrsa, us-department-of-education, trauma-informed-services-in-schools, pediatric-mental-health-care-access-program, florida-mental-health-assistance-allocation]
related_states: [ME, MD, OH, FL]
tags: [school-based-mental-health, financing, telehealth]
---

## Current snapshot

The dominant pattern this week is a reliability gap between state and federal school-mental-health
money. States are stepping up as the more dependable payer — Maryland committed **$96M** in new
state funding for school mental-health services, Ohio's Elyria Schools is deploying **$5.5M** in
state funds for mental health and wellness, and Florida charter schools (Oasis) tapped the state
Mental Health Assistance Allocation. At the federal end, reliability is wobbling: roughly **6,000
Maine students** could lose services because federal funds are frozen in litigation. Non-dilutive
federal money is still available for those who can move quickly — SAMHSA's Trauma-Informed Services
in Schools grant (closes July 16) and HRSA's PMHCA (closes July 10) are open now.
*(as of 2026-W27)*

## Timeline

### 2026-W27 — Maryland commits $96M as Maine's federal-funded services hang in litigation
*Montgomery Community Media · June 29, 2026* · [link](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiwgFBVV95cUxNeGE2clBXdkJMMUxJRjNHVXhmaTJhejdORkpqQTBGUWYxb1gwWHJQN1RtYmRWUmo3a21ZWDRlR21QWFB2RzhDMGRMR3RYei16VV83NnljU3dqU3RMVXJnSU1IXzdSb3R6eWRiVkg1VFAtSUxjU3Z3bEItREpkbHFhWTlUNUNJS1Zxc1N5VDBlR093WFl4MW1Dc1RiR3Y2aXhFeGNCeGdFeWlOcnVGQ2NEWW11Q1BNMmtENDFWYTF5SHZBZw?oc=5)
Maryland leaders announced $96M for school mental-health services. The same week, ~6,000 Maine
students risked losing services as federal funding stayed frozen in a legal fight
(also: [Maine Public, June 29](https://news.google.?oc=5)).

### 2026-W27 — State dollars reach districts and charters; federal grants open
*Cleveland.com · June 30, 2026* · [link](https://news.google.?oc=5)
Ohio's Elyria Schools will use more than $5.5M in state funding for mental health and wellness, and
Florida's Oasis charter schools approved a Mental Health Assistance Plan to access the state
allocation (also: [Cape Coral Breeze, June 26](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMitwFBVV95cUxNcDR4NFBtWGZsR1kzNDQ3ZVpqREVINnZCZ2ZqTVNmWFVlYWhYR2hIdkZOaHJFY0JwX1podzc5WDR0RU5XR20tM09MSl9saE45bzZwV0VaVTAyRndIeTllcFZxOHM3dEdkdVRjZWR3LVJHWVd3ckw4ZXlQakJkbC11N3Q5M3U0M1Nhd3Rwck1RMWRPS0lVSVBCd0ItRVhyQm9sZUhYODZHeWpjRlpMUEk5VnRTVnJ6aEk?oc=5)). Federally, SAMHSA's Trauma-Informed
Services in Schools (closes July 16) and HRSA's PMHCA (closes July 10) are open opportunities.
